message:
core: atomicize AdaptiveController; propagate contention state in condvar relock

Two related concurrency fixes in frankenlibc-core:

1. malloc/elimination.rs — AdaptiveController is shared across the
   per-slot elimination array's hot path (every alloc / free that
   misses the arena-local cache consults it). The previous
   implementation held all its counters as plain `u64` fields
   behind `std::sync::Mutex<AdaptiveController>`, which meant every
   attempt had to acquire a std Mutex just to bump a counter and
   possibly decrement `disabled_remaining`. That's a lot of
   contention on a lock that guards 7 counters and no other state.

   This commit:
   - Swaps `std::sync::{Mutex, MutexGuard}` for
     `parking_lot::{Mutex, MutexGuard}` everywhere in this file. We
     already depend on parking_lot elsewhere and its Mutex is
     strictly better here (no poisoning plumbing, smaller footprint,
     faster uncontended acquire).
   - Rewrites every counter on `AdaptiveController` as
     `AtomicU64`: `observed`, `attempts`, `successes`,
     `window_attempts`, `window_successes`, `disabled_skips`,
     `disabled_remaining`. `AdaptiveController::begin` loses its
     `&mut self` receiver and becomes `&self`; callers no longer
     need to lock to mutate.
   - `disabled_remaining` decrement is now
     `fetch_update(Relaxed, Relaxed, |remaining|
     remaining.checked_sub(1))` so the "we're in a disabled window"
     branch is racy-safe: if two threads both observe remaining==3,
     exactly one of them gets to decrement it to 2; the loser re-enters
     the attempt path.
   - Summary logging gate uses the post-increment value of
     `observed` to detect `SUMMARY_INTERVAL` boundaries, so no
     thread can skip or duplicate a summary row.

   No behavioural regression; the invariants expressed by each
   counter are preserved (observed ≥ attempts ≥ successes, etc.).
   What changes is that the hot path no longer serializes through a
   single Mutex for counter bumps.

2. pthread/cond.rs — `relock_mutex` (the helper that the condvar
   wait/timedwait paths use to re-acquire the caller's mutex on
   wakeup) had a subtle degradation bug. On first iteration it
   tried a plain `compare_exchange(0, 1, ...)` — the standard
   glibc-style 3-state futex protocol where "1 = acquired,
   uncontended" and "2 = acquired, contended". If the CAS failed
   and we parked, then were woken, the retry loop would again try
   to acquire with state `1`, dropping the contended hint. Any
   other waiter arriving between our futex wake and our
   re-acquisition would have to re-announce contention, do another
   futex syscall, and in pathological cases we'd oscillate between
   "contended" and "uncontended" state bits.

   Fix: track a local `observed_contention` flag. On first attempt
   we still go through state 1 (fast path). If that fails, we
   mark contention and park; when we come back around the loop,
   we now try to acquire directly with state 2 (`acquired_state =
   if observed_contention { 2 } else { 1 }`). Subsequent waiters
   see the contended state bit preserved and can bypass the "mark
   contended" CAS that currently follows a failed fast-path
   acquire. No change to the fallback CAS that marks state 1→2
   before parking; that path still runs, which is correct — it's
   only re-acquiring threads that carry the contention hint
   forward.

Neither change is a public API change. Both make the relevant hot
paths behave correctly under actual multi-threaded pressure
instead of relying on accidentally-quiescent workloads to hide
the contention. Paired fuzz / benchmark passes under
`fuzz_malloc_elimination` and the mutex-contention regression
harness are expected to see both reduced syscall counts and more
stable throughput.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
